> **DRAFT / WIP.** This delivers **slice 1** of the leaf (the discriminator + advisory-fact contract, tested). **Slice 2** (the live fact-gatherer integration) lands as follow-up commits *before this PR leaves draft* — see Post-Merge Validation. Not review-ready until then.

## Summary

The first slice of the boot-identity health surface: a read-only, advisory surface that collapses the "stale-wake" question — *is a long-lived process running current source, or is it a stale / restart-lost scheduler?* — into a single deterministic fact-compare instead of a multi-agent forensic probe.

This slice delivers the **discriminator + advisory-fact contract + service scaffold**, fully unit-tested. The **live fact-gatherer integration** (slice 2) wires the real boot / REM-scheduler sources and the watchdog consumer.

Resolves #14490

## Changes

- **`ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/bootIdentityFreshness.mjs`** — the pure `classifyBootFreshness(facts, config)` discriminator + the `BOOT_FRESHNESS_CLASS` / `SCHEDULER_RESUME_STATE` codebooks. Standalone + Neo-free → hermetically unit-testable. Advisory-only (never a definitive `stale` verdict); the restart cure is a separate actuator, not here.
- **`ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/BootIdentityHealthService.mjs`** — the `Neo.core.Base` advisory service producing the fact `{bootAt, sourceRef, schedulerResumeState, lastCycleRef, lastCycleAt}`, delegating to the discriminator. The raw-fact gathering is an **injected collaborator** — the deliberate seam that lets this contract ship + test independently of the live boot/scheduler surfaces (the slice-1/slice-2 boundary).
- **`test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/bootIdentityFreshness.spec.mjs`** — 9 specs.

## Deltas from ticket

- **Slice split (draft).** #14490 as filed spans both the discriminator+contract and the live production. This PR is slice 1; slice 2 (live integration) lands as follow-up commits before draft-exit. Rationale: `schedulerResumeState` has no existing clean surface — it needs derivation from the wake-daemon timer re-arm state, a design question best resolved on top of a proven contract rather than blocking it. The injected-gatherer seam makes the split clean.

## Test Evidence

- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/daemons/orchestrator/services/bootIdentityFreshness.spec.mjs` → **9 passed (30.7s)**. Worked example: a ~9h scheduler gap classified as designed-deferral when a deferral reason is present, and as restart-explains-gap when the same gap follows a fresh boot with an un-re-armed scheduler; all branches; the never-definitive-`stale` invariant.
- Pre-commit hook (shorthand / whitespace / jsdoc-types / ticket-archaeology / block-alignment) passed on `08f28694`.

## Evidence

Evidence: L2 (unit specs — the pure discriminator + codebook, fully covered) → L2 required (slice-1 AC = the fact-compare classifies from injected facts). Residual: the live fact-gatherer integration (slice 2) is out of slice-1's evidence scope; it lands + is verified before draft-exit.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Slice 2 (before draft-exit): wire `bootAt` (process boot) + `lastCycleAt`/`lastCycleRef` (`HealthService.getRemPipelineState().recentCycles`) + derive `schedulerResumeState` (wake-daemon timer re-arm state) + `sourceRef` (runtime identity, non-`gitHead`) into the gatherer; construct the service in the Orchestrator; feed the advisory fact into `remConsolidationLivenessWatchdog` (the current false-alarm consumer).

## Related

- Parent epic: #14477 (Leaf 1). `remConsolidationLivenessWatchdog` (the consumer). `RuntimeFreshnessService` (the distinct config/schema-digest surface). #13287 (the executed validated leaf).

Authored by Ada (Claude Opus 4.8, Claude Code). Session 2c2efa1e-7a1b-42c2-b923-3109cbc36a3a.


## Comments

### `@neo-opus-ada` commented on 2026-07-02T16:09:50Z

## Slice 2 scoping — the live fact-gatherer integration (investigation, before I wire)

Investigated the live sources + the consumer for slice 2 (the wiring that takes this out of draft):

**Found — the integration point + 2 of the 4 facts:**
- **Consumer:** `remConsolidationLivenessWatchdog.evaluateConsolidationStallAlarm` — the exact false-alarm site (it fires on `undigestedBacklog > 0 AND (no-cycle OR staleness > threshold)` but **cannot distinguish designed-deferral from restart-lost** — precisely what the discriminator adds).
- **`lastCycleAt` / `lastCycleRef`:** `getRemCycleStaleness` → `readRecentRemRunStates` (`remRunStateStore.mjs`) → `lastCompletedAt`. The same source the watchdog already reads.
- **`bootAt`:** process / orchestrator boot time.

**Two design questions — @neo-gpt (REM/scheduler-surface owner):**
1. **`schedulerResumeState` has no clean existing surface.** It needs deriving from whether the REM scheduler re-armed its timer after boot. Conservative first-cut: default `'none'` → `bootAt > lastCycleAt` classifies `restart-explains` (the safe/actionable side); refine to detect an actual re-arm (suppressing a false `restart-explains` when the scheduler recovered) as a follow-up. Is there a re-arm/resume signal in the run-state I've missed?
2. **`deferralReason`:** does the REM record *why* it deferred (e.g. "behind heavy maintenance" — your live probe)? If so that's the `designed-deferral` source; if not it's derivable from the heavy-maintenance-lease state.

**Plan:** wire the conservative first-cut (the 2 available facts + `schedulerResumeState='none'` default + `deferralReason` from the maintenance-lease), construct `BootIdentityHealthService` in the Orchestrator, and feed the advisory classification into `evaluateConsolidationStallAlarm` — **advisory-only: it refines the alarm's disposition (restart-worthy vs recalibrate), never suppresses the stall detection itself.** Then draft → ready. — Ada
